rationale_for_baseline_tier: Rapidly progressive bradykinin-mediated (ACE inhibitor) angioedema of the lips and face — the airway region. The airway is currently patent (patient speaking in full sentences, no stridor or breathlessness on report), so this is not baseline T5. However, telehealth cannot examine the oropharynx or larynx, and bradykinin angioedema of this region can progress to laryngeal oedema over hours; the serious mimic (developing laryngeal involvement) genuinely cannot be excluded remotely and would need urgent in-person airway-capable management. This is the narrow telehealth uplift: urgent same-day in-person assessment (T3), with immediate ACE-inhibitor cessation and a low-threshold, mandatory 000 airway safety-net. Remoteness from hospital (~30 minutes) reinforces early escalation if any airway sign appears.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Any voice change, throat tightness or lump-in-throat feeling, difficulty swallowing saliva, tongue swelling, or noisy/difficult breathing
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — call 000 the moment any of these appears
escalation_edges.rationale: These are the heralds of laryngeal oedema and impending airway obstruction; an ambulance provides airway support that neither the patient nor telehealth can supply.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your voice changes, your throat feels tight or you get a lump-in-throat feeling, you can't swallow your saliva, your tongue swells, or your breathing becomes noisy or hard — call 000 for an ambulance right now. Do not drive. If an adrenaline autoinjector is available, use it into your outer thigh while you wait.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Drooling, inability to speak, blue lips, or collapse
escalation_edges.rationale: Signs of critical airway compromise or arrest; 000 with CPR guidance and positioning while paramedics are en route.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you start drooling, can't speak, your lips go blue, or you collapse — this is an emergency. Call 000 immediately and stay on the line; lie the person down and be ready to follow the operator's instructions.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Swelling continues to spread or worsen despite stopping the drug, before the same-day in-person review
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Attend an emergency department the same day if the swelling is still advancing
escalation_edges.rationale: Ongoing progression raises the risk of airway involvement and warrants airway-capable assessment without waiting.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the swelling keeps spreading or getting worse even after stopping the tablet, go to an emergency department today rather than waiting for your appointment.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New features of an allergic reaction appear — widespread hives, wheeze, faintness or dizziness, or vomiting
escalation_edges.rationale: A mixed or evolving anaphylactic picture changes the emergency; adrenaline plus 000 is then indicated.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you suddenly get hives all over, wheezing, feel faint or dizzy, or start vomiting — treat it as a severe allergic reaction: use an adrenaline autoinjector if you have one and call 000 straight away.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: About 30 minutes from the nearest hospital
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower the threshold to activate 000 early; if any airway sign develops, call an ambulance rather than self-transporting, because deterioration in transit would leave the patient without airway support.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Partner is present and can observe overnight
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Ensure the partner knows the specific airway warning signs and to call 000 immediately; the patient should not be left alone tonight.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: The causative ACE inhibitor is part of the patient's regular medication for hypertension
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Confirm the drug is stopped now, flagged as an adverse reaction, and that blood pressure will be re-managed with a non-ACE-inhibitor class at review; caution against ARBs without specialist advice.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Stop the perindopril now and do not take another dose. Because I can't examine your throat over telehealth, you need an urgent in-person assessment today, where a clinician can look at your airway. Keep your partner with you and don't be alone tonight. Call 000 immediately — do not drive — if your voice changes, your throat feels tight or you get a lump-in-throat feeling, you struggle to swallow your saliva, your tongue swells, or your breathing becomes noisy or hard.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent same-day in-person review today (GP urgent appointment, urgent care, or ED); call 000 for an ambulance immediately if any airway warning sign appears.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 is available 24 hours a day. After hours, do not wait for the morning — an urgent same-day face-to-face assessment is needed today, and 000 for any airway change.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document ACE-inhibitor-induced angioedema as an adverse drug reaction; perindopril ceased; needs an alternative antihypertensive class (avoid ACE inhibitors lifelong, ARBs only with caution); consider C4 / C1-esterase-inhibitor testing if angioedema recurs.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treats progressive facial/lip angioedema of the airway region as routine — no same-day in-person airway assessment and no mandatory 000 airway safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Undetected progression to laryngeal oedema and fatal airway obstruction, potentially during transit 30 minutes from hospital.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Recognises the angioedema but relies on next-day GP review only, without same-day in-person assessment or explicit airway escalation triggers.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Dispatches an emergency ambulance immediately for a patient who is currently speaking in full sentences with no airway symptoms and a clear airway on report — 000 should be reserved for the specified airway-warning triggers.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Whether the airway (tongue, pharynx or larynx) is currently involved or about to become involved — this cannot be examined via telehealth.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the airway as unexcludable: cease the ACE inhibitor, arrange urgent same-day in-person airway-capable assessment, and give a low-threshold, mandatory 000 airway safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Being reassured by a currently clear voice and normal breathing and downgrading to routine follow-up, or attempting to confirm airway safety remotely.
